clinical_coherence.notes: 68M Japanese-American with fluctuating cognition, recurrent visual hallucinations, RBD (3 years), symmetric parkinsonism without rest tremor (8 months), orthostatic hypotension, neuroleptic sensitivity to haloperidol. Classic DLB presentation. Exam shows fluctuating attention, visuospatial deficits, myoclonus, symmetric cogwheel rigidity, and orthostatic BP drop of 22mmHg.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG shows fluctuating posterior slowing characteristic of DLB. MRI shows preserved hippocampi (argues against AD). DaTscan abnormal bilateral. Cardiac MIBG reduced (Lewy body pathology). Neuropsych shows severe visuospatial/executive deficits with preserved memory (DLB profile). FDG-PET shows occipital hypometabolism with positive cingulate island sign (pathognomonic for DLB). All biomarkers concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G31.83 (DLB) correct. Differential includes PDD, PD, AD, PSP with appropriate likelihood ratings and the '1-year rule' correctly applied. Critical actions include recognizing DLB core features, stopping haloperidol, and considering cholinesterase inhibitor. Contraindicated actions correctly flag antipsychotics.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Excellent puzzle. PCP diagnosis of PD anchors the agent. Family history of PD in mother, positive DaTscan could mislead. The temporal sequence (cognitive/psychiatric before motor) and neuroleptic sensitivity are key differentiators. Multiple red herrings well-designed.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 68M with RBD, depression, orthostatic hypotension, chronic constipation. Realistic prodromal DLB/synucleinopathy profile.

red_herring_quality.notes: Three well-designed red herrings: PCP's PD diagnosis (anchoring bias), mother's PD (familial suggestion), and positive DaTscan (seems to confirm PD but is positive in DLB too). All are clever, realistic, and properly explained.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding teaching case. Covers DLB core features, 1-year rule for DLB vs PDD, neuroleptic sensitivity as a life-threatening issue, multimodal biomarkers (EEG, DaTscan, MIBG, FDG-PET), and the critical distinction from AD (preserved hippocampi, occipital hypometabolism, cingulate island sign).

strengths: Masterful construction of DLB case within PD condition category
Multimodal biomarker concordance (EEG, MRI, DaTscan, MIBG, FDG-PET, neuropsych)
Excellent red herrings with clear correct interpretations
Neuroleptic sensitivity episode adds clinical urgency
Cingulate island sign on FDG-PET is a sophisticated diagnostic finding
